purpose: Align worker policy, runtime, strategy, and configuration contracts into one coherent security- and performance-aware model.
goal: Remove semantic drift between tool policy defaults, worker taxonomy, runtime selection, and parallelism defaults without introducing compatibility hacks that hide the real contract.

tasks.title: Define a single default semantics for empty ToolPolicy allowlists
acceptance_criteria: ToolPolicy, PreflightGate, MCPAdapter, and all tests agree on the same empty-allowlist behavior.
Legacy permissive behavior exists only through an explicit opt-in field and is documented as legacy.

tasks.title: Audit all tool-policy call sites for implicit open-by-default assumptions
acceptance_criteria: No production code path silently assumes empty allowlists are permissive.
Any existing permissive call site is explicitly marked as legacy or test-only.

tasks.title: Document the tool-policy security contract and migration path
acceptance_criteria: The contract for empty allowlists is documented in repo docs.
The migration note explains how legacy_default_allow is used and when it should not be used.

tasks.title: Update regression tests to validate the new tool-policy contract
acceptance_criteria: Worker API adapter tests and preflight tests assert the same semantics.
No test continues to encode contradictory allowlist expectations.

tasks.title: Separate worker taxonomy by capability and runtime-origin concerns
acceptance_criteria: The worker classification model distinguishes runtime-origin from capability role.
Hermes is no longer ambiguously classified across incompatible policy axes.

tasks.title: Define the exact semantics for external_analysis_only_policy
acceptance_criteria: The policy explicitly states whether Hermes is treated as external, local, or cloud-adjacent.
The allow/deny order is deterministic and matches the policy name.

tasks.title: Normalize worker-kind classification helpers and avoid conflicting categories
acceptance_criteria: Classification helpers cannot simultaneously place a kind into incompatible categories.
policy_allows_kind remains the single authoritative decision point for kind gating.

tasks.title: Align worker-selection service behavior with the new taxonomy contract
acceptance_criteria: Selection decisions fail closed only for intended policy reasons, not because of category drift.
Hermes-related selection behavior is consistent across selection and policy tests.

tasks.title: Add taxonomy-focused regression tests for worker kinds and policies
acceptance_criteria: Hermes classification is tested from both policy and selection perspectives.
Cloud vs external vs local decisions are covered by explicit assertions.

tasks.title: Make WorkerStrategy resilient to partially populated decision objects
acceptance_criteria: Missing reason_codes are treated as an empty list instead of raising TypeError.
Selection decisions with optional metadata do not crash the strategy path.

tasks.title: Introduce a typed selection-decision test builder for WorkerStrategy
acceptance_criteria: Tests use real or builder-backed decision objects rather than fragile naked mocks.
Selected, declined, and no-eligible cases are represented through one shared helper.

tasks.title: Preserve executable vs advisory normalization semantics in WorkerStrategy
acceptance_criteria: Structured tool-call output normalizes to executable.
Prose output remains advisory and empty output remains an explicit decline.

tasks.title: Clarify the strategy fallback contract for no-delegation cases
acceptance_criteria: The reason code for no usable worker output is documented and stable.
The decline path remains diagnostic without pretending work was executed.

tasks.title: Choose the intended ollama parallelism baseline as a product decision
acceptance_criteria: A single authoritative default exists for max_parallel_requests.
The chosen baseline is justified as either throughput-oriented or stability-oriented.

tasks.title: Unify config defaults, runtime config, and schema expectations for parallelism
acceptance_criteria: config/worker_parallelism.default.json, agent/config_defaults.py, and tests all agree.
No hidden fallback reintroduces a different default behind the chosen value.

tasks.title: Reconcile worker-pool concurrency values with ollama model concurrency values
acceptance_criteria: Worker pool limits and ollama limits are internally consistent.
The effective concurrency rule is explained in docs and reflected by tests.

tasks.title: Expand coverage for worker-policy and runtime-target regression cases
acceptance_criteria: The worker-01 shard remains green across policy, selection, and strategy tests.
Regression tests capture the intended contract, not the accidental one.

tasks.title: Add a short design note describing the worker contract model
acceptance_criteria: The design note explains why these issues are coupled and not independent.
The note names the authoritative files for policy, selection, strategy, and config.

tasks.title: Verify the cleaned-up contracts with targeted and shard-level CI runs
acceptance_criteria: Targeted tests pass for tool policy, worker selection, worker strategy, and parallelism schema.
The backend-coverage worker shard no longer exposes the old contract drift.

summary_notes: This track treats the worker failures as a contract-repair problem, not a one-off CI fix.
Empty allowlist semantics, Hermes classification, WorkerStrategy robustness, and parallelism defaults are intentionally grouped because they share the same authority boundary.
The goal is to remove drift between config, runtime code, and tests so the worker subsystem has one authoritative contract.

end_summaries.title: Security contract
items: ToolPolicy defaults are fail-closed unless explicitly opted into legacy permissive behavior.
Preflight and adapter behavior remain aligned with the same empty-allowlist contract.

end_summaries.title: Worker selection contract
items: Hermes and other worker kinds have one unambiguous classification model.
Selection logic, policy logic, and tests all share the same taxonomy.

end_summaries.title: Runtime and performance contract
items: WorkerStrategy no longer fails on partial decision objects.
Parallelism defaults are intentional, documented, and consistent across config sources.
